channel.title: Conference Talks on Vandana Verma
channel.description: Recent content in Conference Talks on Vandana Verma

item.title: Serverless Toronto 2025 - Securing Model Context Protocol (MCP)
item.description: Meetup talk by Vandana Verma on securing Model Context Protocol trust boundaries and MCP-connected systems.

item.title: AI Security Summit - Secure AI
item.description: Talk by Vandana Verma on securing AI systems, added from the public LinkedIn announcement and embedded YouTube session.

item.title: Operation: Defend the North 2025 - Vancouver (siberX)
item.description:
Operation: Defend the North 2025 - Vancouver

Speaking engagement at siberX’s Operation: Defend the North 2025 in Vancouver, Canada. The session continued the Defend the North tour with a focus on practical AppSec, DevSecOps, and security leadership guidance for regional cyber defenders.

Reference:

https://siberx.org/speaker/vandana-verma-sehgal/

item.title: Operation: Defend the North 2025 - Calgary (siberX)
item.description:
Operation: Defend the North 2025 - Calgary

Speaking engagement at siberX’s Operation: Defend the North 2025 in Calgary, Canada. The session focused on modern application security, DevSecOps, and building resilient cyber defence strategies for Canadian enterprises.

Reference:

https://siberx.org/speaker/vandana-verma-sehgal/

item.title: The Cybersecurity Meetup - Exclusive Charter (siberX)
item.description:
The Cybersecurity Meetup - Exclusive Charter

Speaking engagement at siberX’s The Cybersecurity Meetup, a closed-door charter session bringing together security leaders to discuss modern application security, DevSecOps, and leadership challenges in 2025.

Reference:

https://siberx.org/speaker/vandana-verma-sehgal/

item.title: Women in Tech Global Conference 2025 - Implementing Zero Trust in an Organisation to be Uncertainty Ready
item.description:
Session: Implementing Zero Trust in an Organisation to be Uncertainty Ready

Session at the Women in Tech Global Conference 2025 exploring how organisations can operationalise Zero Trust to stay resilient in uncertain times. The talk walked through the principles of Zero Trust, common pitfalls in adoption, and a practical roadmap for security and identity teams.

Key takeaways:

  • Zero Trust as an operating model, not a product
  • Identity, device, and workload as the new perimeter
  • How to incrementally roll out Zero Trust without breaking the business
  • Making Zero Trust investments audit-ready and uncertainty-ready

Reference:

item.title: Black Hat Asia 2025 - Cloud Security: Building Connections in the Cloud
item.description: A Black Hat Asia 2025 meetup session focused on cloud security, identity, compliance, and multi-cloud realities.

item.title: Testflix 2024- Defending the unknown using AI and cybersecurity
item.description:
“Atomic Talk Title: Defending the unknown using AI and cybersecurity”

New and unknown threats emerge daily, making traditional cybersecurity methods insufficient. Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming how we defend against these unknown threats by enabling predictive analysis, real-time monitoring, and automated responses.

AI can detect patterns, anomalies, and suspicious behaviors that humans may overlook, helping to safeguard systems before attacks occur. By combining AI with human expertise, organizations can create a more robust defense strategy, allowing them to identify and neutralize threats faster and more efficiently. This synergy between AI and cybersecurity is essential for staying ahead in the fight against evolving digital dangers.

item.title: Future Tech Summit 2024
item.description:
Title: The Hackers World

Reference:- https://www.bloombloom.co/futureworldsummit-kkem

item.title: Blackhat MEA 2023 - From Vulnerability to Viability: Tackling Supply Chain Challenges Head-On
item.description:
Title: From Vulnerability to Viability: Tackling Supply Chain Challenges Head-On

From Vulnerability to Viability: Tackling Supply Chain Challenges Head-On" dives deep into the pivotal realm of supply chain management, distinguishing between the often conflated concepts of supply chain security and vulnerability. While both elements are crucial to a successful supply chain strategy, they offer different lenses to view potential disruptions and risks. This paper underscores the unique challenges organizations grapple with to ensure robust supply chain security. By offering a comprehensive understanding of the vulnerabilities inherent in global supply chains and presenting strategic measures to address them, this research equips businesses with the tools and insights needed to transition from a state of vulnerability to assured viability.

item.title: Blackhat MEA 2023 - Trust the Process: Optimizing Open Source Security with OWASP Frameworks
item.description:
Title: Trust the Process: Optimizing Open Source Security with OWASP Frameworks

Security has become an integral component, with DevSecOps emerging as a revolutionary approach to integrating security at every development lifecycle phase. This presentation delves deep into the different facets of DevSecOps, illustrating how the OWASP tools bolster each step, ensuring that software is functional and secure by design. Aimed at cybersecurity enthusiasts and professionals, this talk aims to bridge the gap between development and security, underscoring the myriad ways in which OWASP tools can be wielded to fortify applications against the ever-evolving cyber threats

item.title: Defcon - AppSec Village 2023
item.description:
Title: Appsec on a Limited Budget- DEF CON 31- AppSec Village

We are all heading towards the modernization of applications. However, we still see the companies being impacted with the most common website vulnerabilities like SQL Injection, Sensitive data exposure, security misconfiguration, etc. OWASP has many projects which can be tied seamlessly into the application development pipeline structure. However, firstly we don’t know if the projects exist, second, if we know about the projects, we do not know the exact working of the projects. In the talk, I will be talking about how to run an AppSec program with open source projects (OWASP Projects)

item.title: Secure Software Summit 2022 - New Way of Envisioning Security in the Dependencies
item.description: TechStrong Secure Software Summit session by Vandana Verma on dependency security and modern software supply chain risk.

item.title: Apidays 2022 — API Security

Speaker at Apidays conference, presenting on API security topics and best practices for securing APIs in modern application architectures.

https://www.apidays.global/speaker/vandana-verma/

item.title: React Summit 2022 - New Way of Envisioning Security in the Dependencies
item.description: Conference talk by Vandana Verma Sehgal on supply chain thinking and dependency security in modern software development.

item.title: RSA Conference 2022 - Continuous Security - Integrating Pipeline Security
item.description:
Title - Continuous Security - Integrating Pipeline Security

Vandana Verma Sehgal presented this RSAC 2022 session on building security into modern CI/CD workflows.

In a world of continuous delivery and cloud native, boundaries between what an application and what constitutes infrastructure are increasingly blurred. Workloads, the containers they ship in, and platform configuration are often developed and deployed by the same teams, and development velocity is the key metric to success. This session will look at how to build security in the CI/CD process.

item.title: WeAreDevelopers 2022 - Walking into the Era of Supply Chain Risks
item.description: Public conference talk by Vandana Verma Sehgal on software supply chain risks and modern application security realities.

item.title: WeAreDevelopers 2022 - Stranger Danger: Your Java Attack Surface Just Got Bigger
item.description: Public talk by Vandana Verma Sehgal on Java attack surface growth and the security impact of dependency risk.

item.title: RSA Conference 2021 - Let's Talk, Open-Source Application Security
item.description:
RSA Conference 2021 included multiple appearances for Vandana Verma, with the official RSAC presentation archived as “Let’s Talk, Open-Source Application Security.”

Official RSAC presentation

Let’s Talk, Open-Source Application Security

Reference
https://www.rsaconference.com/Library/presentation/USA/2021/lets-talk-open-source-application-security

Additional RSA Conference 2021 appearances

  1. ITSPmagazine podcast interview from the RSAC media section with Keren Elazari
  1. Lightning talk for WeAreHackerz at RSA on “Careers in #AppSec: Lead with Grit”

  1. Birds of a Feather at Career Path (Technical Track) on open source application security in enterprise environments.

Reference: https://www.rsaconference.com/Library/presentation/USA/2021/lets-talk-open-source-application-security

item.title: Grace Hopper Celebration 2021 - India
item.description:
Fri Feb 26, 11:00 AM - 11:45 AM GMT +5.5 (45 Min)

[Track : Security & Privacy]

Title : Redefining Trust in Security

If you ask a security expert on “how to protect their network” they would mostly say “firewalls’’. It may not be enough for today’s environment. The landscape has significantly changed due to the perimeters having disappeared; people can login remotely, cloud has stepped in; everyone is a tenant on a shared server, digital transformation. The mantra of “Trust but verify” is shifted to “Never Trust, always verify”. We firmly believe that the best way to secure your organization is by using context-driven security programs i.e. “Zero Trust”. We will talk about how organizations can adapt to “Zero Trust” with few scenarios on implementation of adaptive controls using existing technologies (Identity & Access Management (IAM), segmentation, analytics, encryption) and governance processes. This paper will be useful for people who want to transform security, who want to think about ubiquitous security and who want to predict ahead than react later.

item.title: Society for Data Science: Spice of Data Analytics in Cyber Security
item.description:
The Society for Data Science team is back with an exciting set of Workshops and Tutorials as a part of ICDMAI 2021 to happen on 15th January 2021.

Join the Workshops on Spice of Data Analytics in Cyber Security by Vandana Verma Sehgal on 15th January 2021.

item.title: Diana Initiative 2020 - Trust, No Trust or Zero Trust - Myth Demystifying
item.description: Talk at The Diana Initiative 2020 exploring the myths around Zero Trust architecture, breaking down the concept of trust models and demystifying common misconceptions about Zero Trust implementation.

item.title: Defcon - AppSec Village 2020
item.description:
Title: Running an appsec program with open source projects

We are all heading towards the modernization of applications. However, we still see the companies being impacted with the most common website vulnerabilities like SQL Injection, Sensitive data exposure, security misconfiguration, etc. OWASP has many projects which can be tied seamlessly into the application development pipeline structure. However, firstly we don’t know if the projects exist, second, if we know about the projects, we do not know the exact working of the projects. In the talk, I will be talking about how to run an AppSec program with open source projects (OWASP Projects)

item.title: Women Tech Network Global Conference: Implementing Zero Trust in an organisation to be uncertainty Ready
item.description:
Session: Implementing Zero Trust in an organisation to be uncertainty Ready

The Current unprecedented situation has changed the IT World forever, no matter what the outcome is, the conceptual changes in the working styles of people are here to stay. The world will never get back to the plane jane everything works only on this building for most of the work environment.

Currently most of the firms have scrambled to enable the workforce to work from home with a certain level of security. This is a band aid fix at best in current time, However the concept of zero trust has been present for ages and is the right kind of solution we need for such situations.

item.title: SACON 2020
Security Guild 2019
item.description: Speaker at Security Guild 2019, presenting on application security and DevSecOps topics to the security community.

item.title: Shifting the DevSecOps Culture, Taking away the sugar piece and giving the pile to ants : Defcon - AppSec Village 2019
item.description:
Title: Shifting the DevSecOps Culture, Taking away the sugar piece and giving the pile to ants

We have been talking about the technical angle of DevSecOps. How do I go about building the DevSecOps culture in the organisation? So far Generally corporates are trying to have all three Plays and teams Dev, Sec and Ops team together. However, the Ideal DevSecOps idea is each individual should know what is happening in the whole process. If person or team has issues/concern, then anyone can stand-up and take the DevSecOps further. Instead of giving the sugar cube to the individuals give them the who Pile of sugar to the Ants (aka teams), incase something fails the other teams can balance the situation or stand up for each other.

item.title: BSides Las Vegas 2019
item.description: Speaker at BSides Las Vegas 2019, part of Hacker Summer Camp alongside DEF CON and Black Hat USA.

item.title: DevSecCon: Cloud Security and the Myths Around it
item.description: The talk would be around the general myths we have on Cloud Security and Cloud Computing. Also, we will map the myths with different roles and discuss how to resolve that.
